Mouse Polling Rate Guide

Polling rate is how often your mouse reports its position to the computer per second. Higher polling rate means smoother and more responsive cursor movement — essential for competitive gaming.

Polling RateIntervalBest ForRating
125 Hz8.0 msCasual / office useFine
500 Hz2.0 msCompetitive gaming (minimum)Good
1000 Hz1.0 msStandard gamingGreat
4000 Hz0.25 msHigh-refresh-rate gamingElite
8000 Hz0.125 msEsports professionalsPro

Frequently Asked Questions

What polling rate does this tool support?
This tool uses a low-overhead architecture with requestAnimationFrame rendering and GPU-composited cursor movement. It can accurately measure polling rates from 125Hz all the way up to 8000Hz without dropping events.
What is a good polling rate for gaming?
500Hz is the competitive minimum. 1000Hz is the standard for most gaming mice. Ultra-competitive players on high-refresh monitors prefer 4000Hz or 8000Hz.
Is higher polling rate always better?
Not necessarily. Above 4000Hz, CPU usage increases slightly. The benefit depends on your monitor refresh rate — a 4000Hz mouse on a 60Hz monitor provides no visible advantage.
Can I increase my mouse polling rate?
Yes, through manufacturer software — Logitech G HUB, Razer Synapse, or SteelSeries Engine — if your mouse hardware supports it. Not all mice can exceed 1000Hz.
